description: 600mA CMOS low-dropout linear regulator with enable function; fixed output voltage options 1.2V, 1.8V, 2.5V, 2.6V, or 3.3V; available in SOT25, SOT89-5, and SO-8 packages.

pins.name: VIN
pins.description: Input supply voltage. SOT25 pin 1. (SO-8 pin 8; SOT89-5 R5 pin 4; SOT89-5 R5A pin 4.)
pins.requirements: Bypass with CIN 1.0μF ceramic (X7R or X5R) as close to pin as possible. VIN must be at least dropout voltage above VOUT for regulation.

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Ground reference. SOT25 pin 2. (SO-8 pins 6 & 7; SOT89-5 R5 pin 2; SOT89-5 R5A pin 2.)
pins.requirements: Connect to circuit ground. SO-8 package has two GND pins (6 and 7) — both must be connected.

pins.name: EN
pins.description: Enable input. Logic HIGH enables regulator output; logic LOW disables output (shutdown). SOT25 pin 3. (SO-8 pin 5; SOT89-5 R5 pin 3; SOT89-5 R5A pin 1.) Has internal 3MΩ pull-down resistor.
pins.requirements: VIH: 1.5V min, 6.0V max. VIL: 0V min, 0.4V max. For always-on operation, tie EN to VIN. Do NOT leave EN floating — internal pull-down will hold it LOW and disable the regulator.

pins.name: NC
pins.description: No connection. SOT25 pin 4. (SO-8 NC pins are 2, 3, 4; SOT89-5 R5 NC is pin 1; SOT89-5 R5A NC is pin 3.)
pins.requirements: Leave unconnected. No special grounding requirement stated.

pins.name: VOUT
pins.description: Regulated output voltage. Fixed at ordered voltage variant (1.2V, 1.8V, 2.5V, 2.6V, or 3.3V). SOT25 pin 5. (SO-8 pin 1; SOT89-5 R5 pin 5; SOT89-5 R5A pin 5.) Auto-discharge to GND via 60Ω when EN is LOW.
pins.requirements: Bypass with COUT 1.0μF ceramic (X7R or X5R) as close to pin as possible. Maximum output current 600mA. Output discharges to GND through 60Ω when disabled.

required_passives.placement: VIN to GND, as close to VIN pin as possible
required_passives.reason: CIN — input bypass capacitor. Required for stability and transient response. Recommended X7R or X5R dielectric.

required_passives.placement: VOUT to GND, as close to VOUT pin as possible
required_passives.reason: COUT — output bypass capacitor. Required for stability. Stable with ceramic, tantalum, and aluminum electrolytic. Recommended X7R or X5R dielectric if ceramic.

application_circuits.name: Typical Application Circuit (p.2)
application_circuits.description: Power path: VIN source → CIN (1μF ceramic, VIN to GND) → VIN pin (SOT25 pin 1) → [AP2112] → VOUT pin (SOT25 pin 5) → COUT (1μF ceramic, VOUT to GND) → load. Enable path: VEN logic signal → EN pin (SOT25 pin 3); tie EN to VIN for always-on operation. When EN is HIGH regulator is ON; when EN is LOW regulator is OFF and VOUT discharges to GND via internal 60Ω resistor.

common_mistakes: EN pin has internal 3MΩ pull-down resistor — if EN is left floating, the regulator will be OFF. For always-on use, tie EN directly to VIN.
EN logic: H (≥1.5V) = regulator ON; L (≤0.4V) = regulator OFF and output discharged. Do NOT rely on floating EN for ON state.
Input/output capacitors: It is RECOMMENDED to use X7R or X5R dielectric capacitor if 1.0μF ceramic is selected (Note 4).
SO-8 package has two GND pins (pins 6 and 7) — both must be connected to ground.
VIN must exceed VOUT by at least the dropout voltage for regulation. At 600mA load, dropout is 250mV typ (3.3V variant) — ensure VIN ≥ VOUT + dropout.
Absolute Maximum VIN is 6.5V — exceeding this causes permanent damage.

Thermal shutdown occurs at +160°C junction temperature. At high output current with high VIN-VOUT differential, manage PCB thermal dissipation. θJA = 184°C/W (SOT25), 120°C/W (SOT89-5), 114°C/W (SO-8).
Stresses greater than those listed under Absolute Maximum Ratings may cause permanent damage to the device (Note 5).
The AP2112 is a FIXED output voltage device — output voltage is set at ordering (1.2V, 1.8V, 2.5V, 2.6V, or 3.3V). There are no external feedback resistors.
SOT89-5 comes in two incompatible pin-out options (R5 and R5A) — verify which option is ordered before PCB layout. Pin 1 is EN on R5A but NC on R5; pin 3 is EN on R5 but NC on R5A.
notes: Multi-package device — pin numbering differs significantly across packages. SOT25 pin mapping (canonical): 1=VIN, 2=GND, 3=EN, 4=NC, 5=VOUT. SO-8 pin mapping: 1=VOUT, 2=NC, 3=NC, 4=NC, 5=EN, 6=GND, 7=GND, 8=VIN. SOT89-5 R5: 1=NC, 2=GND, 3=EN, 4=VIN, 5=VOUT. SOT89-5 R5A: 1=EN, 2=GND, 3=NC, 4=VIN, 5=VOUT. ALWAYS verify package variant before routing PCB. Output auto-discharge feature (60Ω to GND when disabled) means VOUT will bleed down when EN is LOW — factor this into power sequencing designs.

layout_example.title: AP2112K-3.3 Layout Example
layout_notes: Place CIN (1�F ceramic) as close as possible to VIN pin (pad 1) � within 1mm. Short VIN trace minimises input impedance.
Place COUT (1�F ceramic) as close as possible to VOUT pin (pad 5) � within 1mm. Long output traces can cause instability.
EN (pad 3) is tied directly to VIN with a short trace routed below the IC. Internal 3MO pull-down means EN must never float.
GND pin (pad 2) should connect to a solid ground plane with a short, direct trace or via. Low-inductance GND is essential for LDO stability.
Use X7R or X5R ceramic capacitors for CIN and COUT. Avoid Y5V/Z5U � their capacitance drops sharply with DC bias and temperature.
NC (pad 4) may be left open or connected to GND for extra solder mechanical stability. No electrical requirement.
Foldback short-circuit current is only 50mA � keep load wiring resistance low so startup is not hindered.
Thermal resistance ?JA = 184�C/W (SOT25). At 600mA / 1.7V dropout: 1.02W ? ?T = 188�C over ambient. Keep dropout low (use 3.3V?3.3V or limit current at high VIN-VOUT differential).
Output auto-discharges to GND through internal 60O when EN is LOW � account for this in power-sequencing designs.
Maximum input voltage is 6.0V recommended (6.5V absolute). Do not exceed.

test_circuit_components.placement: Within 1mm of U1 pad 1 (VIN). Place close and connect with short, wide traces to both VIN rail and GND plane.
Within 1mm of U1 pad 5 (VOUT). Place close and connect with short trace to VOUT pin and direct path to GND plane.

layout_example.thermal_notes: ?JA = 184�C/W (SOT25). At 600mA output with 1.7V dropout: Pdiss = 1.02W ? ?Tja = 188�C. To stay below 85�C junction at 25�C ambient, limit power dissipation to ~0.33W (e.g. 100mA at 3.3V from 5V). Use copper pours on GND net under and around IC for spreading.
